title: OpenClaw → Swarmstr port (Nostr-first)
Phase 0: Foundation scaffold
Define event kind/tag model and envelopes
Implement bootstrap config loader and validation
Scaffold CLI/daemon entrypoints
Phase 1: Relay runtime + DM loop
Implement relay manager (connect/reconnect/sub/pub)
Implement inbound DM subscription and decryption
Implement outbound DM send path
Phase 2: Nostr state persistence + DM policy
Implement Nostr-backed replaceable state store
Persist config/lists/sessions to Nostr
Implement DM policy modes
Phase 3: Transcript + agent integration
Persist transcript entries as parameterized docs
Integrate agent runtime loop
Add idempotency/checkpoint recovery
Phase 4: Nostr memory + local index
Design memory event schema and retrieval tags
Build local searchable cache/index
Nostr checkpoint sync for index
Phase 5: Optional admin API + parity
Implement optional local admin API
Add OpenClaw parity surface incrementally
Operational hardening and observability
Parity phase B: agents/models/tools/skills surfaces
description: Implement OpenClaw gateway parity for agent, agent.wait, agent.identity.get, agents.* CRUD/file methods, models.list, tools.catalog, and skills status/install/update endpoints. Preserve Swarmstr Nostr-native state persistence for agent/session artifacts.

title: Parity phase C: node/device pairing, cron, exec approvals, wizard, talk/voicewake/tts
description: Implement OpenClaw parity method families for node/device pairing and token lifecycle, node invoke/event/result/canvas capability, cron scheduling/status/runs, exec approval workflows, wizard flow, talk.mode/config, voicewake, tts providers/config/convert, and secrets reload/resolve.

title: Websocket event-stream parity and contract alignment
description: Align Swarmstr WS event catalog and payload contracts with OpenClaw expectations (agent/chat/presence/tick/health/heartbeat/cron/pairing/update-available) while preserving Nostr-backed control semantics and existing connect-policy hardening.

title: Channel/plugin compatibility profile for parity boundary
description: Define and implement a compatibility profile for OpenClaw channel/plugin-dependent gateway methods in Swarmstr. Ship explicit supported/not-supported responses and adapters for core channel status/logout surfaces without introducing full plugin runtime parity yet.

title: Parity matrix, OpenClaw fixture tests, and CI gating
description: Build a living parity matrix (OpenClaw method/event/auth semantics vs Swarmstr), add cross-implementation fixture tests for WS + RPC + auth + precondition semantics, and gate merges on parity suite deltas.

title: Complete swarmstr-9 remaining deep parity semantics
description: Follow-up for swarmstr-9: implement non-stub semantics for logs.tail/channels.status/channels.logout/usage.status/usage.cost, add full sessions mutation methods (sessions.patch/reset/delete/compact), and wire chat.abort to real runtime cancellation behavior.
notes: Completed non-stub runtime semantics for logs.tail/channels.status/channels.logout/usage.status/usage.cost across both Nostr control RPC and admin /call. Wired daemon runtime callbacks (log ring buffer, channel runtime state, usage tracker), propagated control call accounting + abort accounting, and added tests in cmd/swarmstrd/main_test.go and internal/admin/server_test.go. Full go test ./... passing.

title: Post-port enhancements (security + parity + runtime depth)
Encrypt persisted Nostr docs (NIP-44 target)
Add envelope encryption/decryption primitives for state/transcript/memory
Migrate docs repos to encrypted payloads with compatibility fallback
Harden metadata leakage policy for tags/session/source
Richer agent runtime integration
Replace echo runtime with provider-backed agent loop
Add tool-call execution and transcript wiring
close_reason: Implemented tool execution in provider runtime and persisted tool traces into transcript entries

title: Add per-session context assembly using memory index
close_reason: Added per-session memory context assembly from memory index and wired it into turn processing

title: Expanded OpenClaw parity methods
Define gateway-style method surface and request/response schemas
close_reason: Defined canonical gateway method schemas and decoders, including object and positional params

title: Implement first parity bundle (status/config/chat/memory methods)
close_reason: Implemented parity method handlers for status/config/chat/session/memory (plus list methods) on admin and control surfaces

title: Add compatibility tests against OpenClaw behavior contracts
close_reason: Added compatibility-focused tests for method decoding and dispatch behavior

title: Nostr-native control/data plane convergence
description: Next wave: strengthen relay-native semantics for control/auth/state convergence and persistent coordination over Nostr.

title: State/list convergence and relay conflict handling
description: Define deterministic merge semantics for replaceable docs/lists across relays and expose list state operations for control surfaces.

title: Define convergence semantics for replaceable state docs
description: Specify and enforce deterministic winner selection/tie-breaking and stale-write safeguards for config/list/session docs.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic replaceable-event winner semantics (created_at then ID) and added convergence unit tests

title: Implement optimistic state version checks for config/list writes
description: Add optional expected-version/expected-event preconditions to prevent accidental overwrite from stale callers.
close_reason: Added optimistic write preconditions (expected_version/expected_event) for config.put and list.put across control and admin method surfaces

title: Add conformance tests for multi-relay state convergence
description: Add unit tests validating deterministic behavior under out-of-order relay observations.
close_reason: Added conformance tests for deterministic event ordering and precondition conflict behavior

title: Control RPC per-caller rate limiting
description: Add lightweight in-memory abuse controls to limit rapid control RPC bursts from a single caller.

title: Implement per-caller control RPC throttle
description: Track recent caller activity and reject requests that exceed configured minimum interval.
close_reason: Implemented per-caller control RPC throttle with configurable minimum interval and dedicated rate-limit error code

title: Add throttle tests/docs
description: Validate throttle decisions and document behavior/knobs.
close_reason: Added throttle decision tests and README documentation

title: Relay policy publication and runtime adoption
description: Persist and apply read/write relay policy updates from Nostr docs with safe runtime reload semantics.

title: Define relay policy update contract and validation rules
description: Specify how relay policy docs are validated, normalized, and rejected when unsafe/invalid.
close_reason: Implemented relay policy normalization/validation contract (ws/wss only, non-empty read/write sets, dedupe) and tests

title: Apply relay policy changes to control + DM buses at runtime
description: Wire config relay changes into runtime subscriptions/publication targets without restart where feasible.
close_reason: Applied relay policy updates to DM/control runtime publish paths via live SetRelays updates

title: Add tests for relay policy persistence and adoption
description: Validate that updated relay policies persist and are reflected in runtime behavior.
close_reason: Added tests covering relay policy normalization/validation and runtime relay-set behavior

title: Runtime relay introspection and control-plane visibility
description: Expose effective relay policy/state over control methods so operators can verify live routing behavior.

title: Add relay.policy.get control/admin method
description: Return persisted read/write relay policy and current runtime publish relay sets.
close_reason: Implemented relay.policy.get on control/admin surfaces with schema + handler support

title: Wire runtime relay snapshots from DM/control buses
description: Expose current bus relay sets via stable accessors for introspection.
close_reason: Exposed stable runtime relay snapshots via DMBus.Relays and ControlRPCBus.Relays

title: Add tests/docs for relay policy introspection
description: Cover method decoding/dispatch and daemon handler output contract.
close_reason: Added tests and docs for relay policy introspection contract

title: Optimistic concurrency ergonomics and conflict diagnostics
description: Improve optimistic write conflict handling with structured diagnostics for operators and callers.

title: Return detailed precondition conflict metadata
description: Include current version/event in conflict errors for config/list optimistic writes.
close_reason: Added detailed optimistic precondition conflict diagnostics (resource/expected/current version+event)

title: Add conflict-focused tests for control and admin method surfaces
description: Verify conflict diagnostics and status behavior for config.put and list.put.
close_reason: Added control/admin conflict tests validating status and diagnostic metadata

title: NIP-86 response compatibility hardening
description: Improve NIP-86 profile fidelity with structured error objects and stable error codes for control calls.

title: Add structured NIP-86 error model
description: Implement code/message/data error mapping for admin /call NIP-86 responses.
close_reason: Implemented structured NIP-86 error objects with stable code mapping and conflict data support

title: Add tests/docs for NIP-86 structured errors
description: Validate error mapping and document object format.
close_reason: Added tests/docs for NIP-86 structured error behavior

title: Nostr control RPC error envelope standardization
description: Align control RPC relay responses with structured error objects including stable codes and diagnostic data.

title: Add typed error propagation for control RPC bus
description: Carry code/data from handler errors into kind-38386 responses.
close_reason: Implemented typed error propagation (code/data) through control RPC bus and structured kind-38386 error envelopes

title: Add tests/docs for structured control RPC errors
description: Validate control bus error payload shape and document response contract.
close_reason: Added tests and docs for structured control RPC error payloads

title: Control RPC input hardening and abuse resistance
description: Harden inbound Nostr control RPC parsing/validation with strict decoding and payload size limits.

title: Enforce strict control request decoding
description: Reject unknown fields and malformed control request envelopes before handler dispatch.
close_reason: Implemented strict control request decoding with unknown-field rejection and method normalization

title: Add payload size limits and tests for control RPC
description: Bound control request content size and add tests/docs for rejection behavior.
close_reason: Added request size limit (64KiB) plus tests/docs for control RPC rejection behavior

title: DM transport payload hardening and limits
description: Add defensive input/output limits for DM plaintext handling to reduce abuse and malformed payload risk.

title: Enforce inbound/outbound DM plaintext limits
description: Validate DM plaintext length/content before processing or publishing.
close_reason: Enforced DM plaintext sanitization/limits for inbound processing and outbound publishing

title: Add DM payload hardening tests/docs
description: Cover rejection behavior and document size/normalization limits.
close_reason: Added DM payload hardening tests and README documentation

title: Transcript and memory payload guardrails
description: Add bounded persistence limits for transcript and memory documents to prevent oversized event payloads.

title: Enforce transcript text/meta limits before persistence
description: Validate and bound transcript entry text/meta field sizes in repository writes.
close_reason: Enforced transcript entry text/meta limits during persistence writes

title: Enforce memory text/keywords/meta limits and add tests
description: Bound memory doc payload size and add unit tests/docs for rejection behavior.
close_reason: Enforced memory payload limits (text/topic/keywords/meta) and added guardrail tests/docs

title: OpenClaw TypeScript parity program (Swarmstr Go)
description: Structured roadmap to close remaining functional gaps between swarmstr Go and current openclaw TypeScript gateway/runtime surfaces.

title: Parity blockers and contract mismatches
description: Resolve currently-known contract breaks that prevent parity-safe operation.

title: Implement signer_url bootstrap runtime path
description: Add signer abstraction and runtime flow so configs validated with signer_url are executable in daemon/CLI.
close_reason: Implemented signer_url runtime resolution (env/file/direct key) and wired daemon/CLI to execute without private_key-only requirement

title: Make status relay reporting live instead of startup snapshot
description: Source relays for /status and status.get from current runtime/config state after config.put updates.
close_reason: Implemented live relay status sourcing via StatusRelays callback for /status and status.get, wired to runtime config state

title: Replace byte slicing with UTF-8-safe truncation in request/context paths
description: Use rune-aware truncation for memory search query normalization, HTTP query capping, and provider context caps.
close_reason: Replaced byte slicing with UTF-8-safe truncation in admin query path, gateway normalize path, and provider context cap; added tests

title: Gateway protocol and websocket control-plane parity
description: Implement OpenClaw-like gateway protocol frames, websocket lifecycle, and auth/connect behaviors.

title: Define protocol schema/version + frame contracts
description: Port request/response/event frame schema patterns and versioning semantics from OpenClaw protocol schema set.
close_reason: Added gateway protocol package with protocol version negotiation, strict req/res/event frame contracts, connect/hello shapes, schema name registry, and tests

title: Implement websocket gateway runtime with auth/challenge and presence events
description: Add ws connection manager, connect flow, subscriptions, and server-side event fanout parity.
close_reason: Integrated gateway WS runtime into swarmstrd with method routing to runtime state, connect challenge+nonce auth flow, presence event fanout, strict protocol negotiation, and test coverage

title: Add connection hardening (unauthorized flood guard/rate limits)
description: Port OpenClaw-style connect-policy and unauthorized flood protections to gateway websocket runtime.
notes: Validated that unauthorized flood guard and handshake/auth rate limiting are implemented in internal/gateway/ws/runtime.go: per-IP handshake limiter (AuthRateLimitPerMin via allowHandshake), per-connection unauthorized burst tracking/close (UnauthorizedBurstMax via bumpUnauthorized/shouldClose), and trusted-proxy auth gating. Verified with focused ws tests passing.

title: Core method surface parity (health/config/sessions/chat/logs/channels/usage)
description: Expand method coverage toward OpenClaw's operational core methods and semantics.

title: Implement health/doctor/logs/channels/usage method bundle
description: Add health, doctor.memory.status, logs.tail, channels.status/logout, usage.status/cost equivalents.
notes: Executed directly. Method bundle is implemented across schema + admin /call + Nostr control RPC for: health, doctor.memory.status, logs.tail, channels.status, channels.logout, usage.status, usage.cost. Runtime semantics are wired (log buffer, channel state, usage tracker) and verified with targeted tests: TestHandleControlRPCRequest_OperationalSemantics and TestDispatchMethodCallRuntimeCallbacks.

title: Implement config.set/apply/patch/schema semantics
description: Support config mutation patterns and schema/introspection methods aligned with OpenClaw gateway behavior.
notes: Executed directly. Verified config.set/apply/patch/schema semantics are wired across schema + admin /call + Nostr control RPC. Added/expanded tests for control/admin dispatch and config mutation helpers (cmd/swarmstrd/main_test.go, internal/admin/server_test.go, internal/gateway/methods/config_mutation_test.go). go test ./... passing.

title: Implement sessions and chat parity bundle
description: Add sessions.list/preview/patch/reset/delete/compact and chat.history/chat.abort parity behaviors.
notes: Executed directly. Verified sessions/chat parity methods are implemented across schema + admin /call + Nostr control RPC for sessions.list/sessions.preview/sessions.patch/sessions.reset/sessions.delete/sessions.compact/chat.history/chat.abort. Added explicit coverage for list/preview/history in both control and admin tests (cmd/swarmstrd/main_test.go, internal/admin/server_test.go). Full go test ./... passing.

title: Agent/model/skills/tools parity
description: Port OpenClaw agent, model catalog, skills management, and tools catalog method families.

title: Implement agent + agent.wait + identity method semantics
description: Port core agent invocation/wait workflows and identity retrieval behaviors.
notes: Executed directly. Implemented agent method bundle with OpenClaw-aligned names and semantics: agent (accepted run with run_id), agent.wait (wait/timeout with status payload), and agent.identity.get. Wired across schema decoding/normalization + supported methods + admin /call dispatch + Nostr control RPC dispatch. Added in-memory agent run registry and asynchronous run execution path in swarmstrd runtime semantics. Added tests for method decode/support and both control/admin dispatch flows. Full go test ./... passing.

title: Implement agents CRUD and file management methods
description: Add agents.list/create/update/delete and agents.files.list/get/set with persistence contracts.
close_reason: Implemented agents CRUD/files methods across schema/admin/control with Nostr docs persistence and tests

title: Implement models.list + tools.catalog + skills status/install/update
description: Port discovery and management methods for models/tools/skills.
close_reason: Implemented models.list, tools.catalog, and skills status/install/update across schema/admin/control with tests

title: Node/device/pairing/cron/exec/security method parity
description: Bring parity for node and device control plane, cron automation, approvals, and secret handling methods.

title: Implement node/device pairing and token lifecycle methods
description: Port node.pair.*, device.pair.*, and device.token rotate/revoke flows with authorization checks.
close_reason: Implemented node/device pairing and token lifecycle methods, then hardened semantics and payload shapes toward OpenClaw parity (request upsert, repair semantics, sorted lists, token summary redaction, rotate/revoke rules, verify behavior) with passing tests

title: Implement node.invoke/event/result and cron method families
description: Add node.invoke pipeline plus cron.list/status/add/update/remove/run/runs behavior.
notes: Implemented node.invoke/node.event/node.result plus cron.list/status/add/update/remove/run/runs across all control surfaces. Added new method constants, request schemas, normalization and decoders in internal/gateway/methods/schema.go (including positional support where applicable). Wired admin /call dispatch and server option callbacks for all new methods. Added daemon runtime registries for node invocation lifecycle and cron job/run state, and integrated handlers in handleControlRPCRequest plus admin runtime callback wiring. Added parity tests in schema_test.go, admin/server_test.go, and cmd/swarmstrd/main_test.go. Validation passed: go test ./internal/gateway/methods ./internal/admin ./cmd/swarmstrd.
close_reason: Completed: implemented node invoke/event/result and cron method families with tests

title: Implement exec approvals, secrets, wizard, talk/voicewake/tts bundles
description: Port remaining operational method families required for OpenClaw gateway surface parity.
notes: Implemented exec approvals, secrets, wizard, talk, voicewake, and tts method bundles across schema, admin /call dispatch, and Nostr control RPC dispatch. Added in-memory runtime registries for approvals/pending resolutions, wizard session lifecycle, and talk/voicewake/tts operational state. Wired daemon admin callbacks and control handlers, added decode/dispatch/control tests, and validated with go test ./internal/gateway/methods ./internal/admin ./cmd/swarmstrd.
close_reason: Completed: implemented operational bundle methods with runtime wiring and tests

title: Parity conformance and regression harness
description: Establish executable parity checks against OpenClaw contracts and method schema fixtures.
notes: Completed through child tasks 4.6.1/4.6.2/4.6.3: parity matrix artifact + acceptance tests, behavioral parity tests for auth/rate-limit/conflict semantics (including expected_version=0 control-plane fix), and CI gating workflow/suite to prevent regressions.

title: Build method-by-method parity matrix and acceptance tests
description: Track implemented vs expected methods, params, result shapes, and error semantics with automated checks.
notes: Added executable parity matrix artifact sourced from OpenClaw gateway BASE_METHODS (docs/parity/gateway-method-parity.json, mirrored to internal/gateway/methods/testdata/parity/gateway-method-parity.json). Added acceptance test internal/gateway/methods/parity_matrix_test.go to enforce matrix consistency: unique method rows, valid status taxonomy, summary count integrity, and implemented/partial mapping validity against SupportedMethods(). Current snapshot summary: 94 OpenClaw base methods, 78 implemented, 1 partial alias, 15 missing.
close_reason: Completed parity matrix + acceptance test harness

title: Add cross-impl behavioral tests for auth, rate-limit, and conflict semantics
description: Ensure Swarmstr matches OpenClaw behavior for critical control-plane edge cases.
notes: Added parity-focused behavioral tests for control-plane edge cases and closed a discovered mismatch: (1) cmd/swarmstrd/main_test.go now validates expected_version=0 create-if-missing + conflict-on-existing semantics in control RPC list.put path; (2) internal/gateway/ws/runtime_test.go now validates HTTP 429 behavior at handshake endpoint once auth rate-limit threshold is exceeded; (3) internal/gateway/methods/nip86_test.go now asserts unauthorized/forbidden -> -32001 and method-not-found -> -32601 mappings. During execution, tests exposed control-RPC precondition drift vs admin path for expected_version=0; fixed in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go for both list.put and config.put by honoring ExpectedVersionSet and explicit zero semantics consistently.
close_reason: Completed behavioral parity tests + fixed control precondition semantics drift

title: Gate future changes on parity CI suite
description: Integrate parity test suite into CI to prevent regressions while continuing implementation.
notes: Added CI parity gate workflow at .github/workflows/parity.yml and canonical parity gate script at scripts/ci-parity.sh. Workflow now runs on push and pull_request, provisions Go from go.mod, and executes focused parity tests for method-matrix integrity, NIP-86 error mapping, WS auth/rate-limit behavior, and control/admin expected_version=0 precondition semantics. Added README section documenting local parity gate execution command. Validated locally via bash ./scripts/ci-parity.sh with all suites passing.

title: Complete websocket control-plane parity gaps
description: Follow-up from swarmstr-4.2.2: add explicit client subscription model, request method/event routing integration with daemon state, unauthorized flood guard/rate limits, and connect policy parity to OpenClaw semantics.
close_reason: Completed websocket control-plane parity gap set through swarmstr-6 and swarmstr-7: subscription model, request routing integration, connect-policy parity, trusted proxy controls, and cryptographic device signature verification.

title: Add device-identity and trusted-proxy connect policy parity
description: Continue swarmstr-5: implement OpenClaw-style connect-policy semantics for ws handshake (role/device identity requirements, trusted proxy allowances, and richer auth decision reasons).
close_reason: Added trusted-proxy auth allowances, role/device connect-policy checks (node + remote control-ui), richer auth reason/detail codes, and expanded tests

title: Implement cryptographic device signature verification in WS connect
description: Follow-up from swarmstr-6: current device policy enforces presence/nonce/timestamp but does not cryptographically verify ConnectDevice.signature against ConnectDevice.publicKey and signed payload semantics.
close_reason: Implemented cryptographic device signature verification for WS connect (device public key parsing, device-id derivation/match, v3+v2 payload verification, and rejection detail codes), plus tests for valid signature and mismatch cases.

title: Execute parity wave toward OpenClaw gateway feature coverage
description: Execution-focused roadmap to move Swarmstr from foundational parity to broad OpenClaw gateway parity, while keeping Nostr as primary transport/state backbone.

title: Parity phase A: operational/config/session/chat methods
description: Implement OpenClaw method bundle parity for health, doctor.memory.status, logs.tail, channels.status/logout, usage.status/cost, config.set/apply/patch/schema, sessions.list/preview/patch/reset/delete/compact, and chat.history/chat.abort. Ensure both /call and Nostr control ingress use identical behavior.
notes: Completed next high-leverage parity slice: optimistic precondition semantics now treat expected_version=0 as explicit create-if-missing (instead of implicit unset) for both list.put and config.put. Implementation details: added explicit ExpectedVersionSet presence tracking to ListPutRequest/ConfigPutRequest; updated object and positional precondition decoding to preserve presence; updated normalization to validate negatives only when explicitly set; updated dispatch precondition checks to use presence and enforce expected_version=0 conflict/success semantics against current existence for both list and config paths. Added tests for decoder presence tracking and runtime behavior (missing succeeds, existing conflicts) in schema_test.go and server_test.go. Validation: go test ./internal/gateway/methods ./internal/admin ./cmd/swarmstrd passed.

title: Drop-in parity closeout (execution-first)
description: Fastest path to true OpenClaw drop-in parity: close remaining gateway method gaps, enforce payload-shape/semantic parity, then finish skills/extensions/runtime-tooling parity with Nostr-native persistence semantics.

title: Close remaining gateway method contract gaps (94/94)
description: Implement remaining missing methods and aliases in schema/admin/control runtime, with tests and parity matrix updates.

title: Implement node surface parity bundle
description: Add node.list, node.describe, node.rename, node.canvas.capability.refresh, and node.invoke.result alias semantics across methods/admin/control/runtime with tests.
notes: Completed: implemented node.list/node.describe/node.rename/node.canvas.capability.refresh and node.invoke.result alias across schema decode+normalize, supported method list, admin dispatch, control-RPC dispatch, runtime apply handlers, and test coverage (schema/admin/control). Updated parity matrix entries for this bundle and validated with go test ./internal/gateway/methods ./internal/admin ./cmd/swarmstrd.

title: Implement operational missing methods bundle
description: Add tts.setProvider, exec.approval.waitDecision, skills.bins, update.run, last-heartbeat, set-heartbeats, wake, system-presence, system-event, send, browser.request across all control surfaces with tests.
notes: Completed: implemented tts.setProvider, exec.approval.waitDecision, skills.bins, update.run, last-heartbeat, set-heartbeats, wake, system-presence, system-event, send, and browser.request across schema decode+normalize, supported methods, admin dispatch, control-RPC dispatch, runtime apply/state helpers, parity matrix updates, and tests.

title: Payload-shape + semantic parity hardening
description: Capture OpenClaw request/response/error fixtures for high-risk methods and assert Swarmstr behavior matches shape/semantics at admin and control ingress.
notes: Completed: fixture-driven parity hardening for high-risk method semantics, envelope semantics (HTTP vs NIP-86), NIP-86 error-code mapping, and precondition conflict data payloads across admin and control ingress tests.

title: Skills + extensions parity with Nostr persistence
description: Implement OpenClaw-equivalent skills lifecycle and extension/plugin contracts, persisted via Nostr docs/lists, including install/update/runtime discovery parity.
notes: In progress: tools/skills parity slices are being implemented with OpenClaw-compatible payload aliases and core tool taxonomy, while retaining Nostr-backed config/list persistence.

title: Skills runtime parity and bins/install semantics
description: Match OpenClaw skills runtime inventory, bin exposure, install/update status transitions, and persisted config shape.
notes: In progress: added stricter OpenClaw-style skills response contracts. skills.status now returns workspaceDir/managedSkillsDir/skills report shape, skills.install returns {ok,message,stdout,stderr,code}, and skills.update returns {ok,skillKey,config}. Normalized skills.update skill keys to lowercase, ignored blank env keys (OpenClaw semantics), aligned unknown-agent handling semantics, and made skills.bins global (no agent-scoped params). Also hardened skills.update key migration semantics to collapse mixed-case legacy keys into canonical lowercase storage and prevent duplicate entries.

title: Extension/tooling parity for agent codegen/self-heal
description: Port extension lifecycle contracts and tool exposure so Swarmstr agents have OpenClaw-equivalent code generation and self-healing/modification capabilities.
notes: In progress: tools.catalog now follows OpenClaw section taxonomy and camelCase payload semantics (agentId/pluginId/defaultProfiles), with plugin conflicts suppressed against core IDs and includePlugins behavior preserved. Added extension gateway method projection into supportedMethods (from extensions.entries[*].gateway_methods / gatewayMethods, enabled-only), keeping plugin-distributed control surfaces discoverable. Completed hardening for plugin tools persisted as []string (not only []any) and aligned admin supportedmethods to use runtime extension-aware method projection. Added plugin config lifecycle parity keys in config schema/mutation paths: plugins.deny, plugins.load, plugins.load.paths aliases, plugins.entries.<id>.apiKey/env, and plugins.installs metadata paths, plus patch-time env merge semantics and installs field validation. New runtime policy gating now applies consistently to both extension methods and plugin tool catalog exposure (plugins.enabled, plugins.load, allow/deny, per-entry enabled). Latest slices: implemented install-record lifecycle normalization for plugins.installs (canonical sourcePath/installPath aliases, source enum validation, required source on object writes, installedAt auto-stamping on create/update, and deletion semantics when source/record is cleared), plus runtime lifecycle helpers ApplyPluginInstallOperation / ApplyPluginUninstallOperation / ApplyPluginUpdateOperation with OpenClaw-aligned config effects and outcome taxonomy, backed by focused tests. Latest slice: wired plugins.install/plugins.uninstall/plugins.update across schema + admin dispatch + Nostr control RPC with config persistence and not-found semantics. Next slice completed: added runtime backend semantics (sourcePath/spec validation, installPath defaults, safe uninstall file deletion for managed paths, and pinned-version unchanged behavior for npm updates).

title: Implement runtime extension install operation parity
description: Add runtime install operation semantics that mirror OpenClaw plugin install lifecycle: create/enable entry, persist install record metadata, and update allow/load path contracts safely.
notes: Implemented runtime install lifecycle parity helper (ApplyPluginInstallOperation): upserts normalized install records, optionally enables plugin entries, links path installs into load_paths with dedupe, and preserves installedAt semantics. Added focused tests for enablement, normalization, timestamp stamping, and idempotent load path behavior.

title: Implement runtime extension uninstall operation parity
description: Port OpenClaw uninstall lifecycle semantics: remove plugin entry/install/allowlist/load-path links and reset slot references while preserving unrelated config state.
notes: Implemented ApplyPluginUninstallOperation with OpenClaw-style config cleanup: removes plugin entry/install records, prunes allow list and path-linked load_paths, resets memory slot to memory-core, preserves unrelated plugin state, and returns action flags. Added coverage for full cleanup and missing-plugin error behavior.

title: Implement runtime extension update operation parity
description: Port OpenClaw update lifecycle semantics for npm-installed plugins: target filtering, status outcomes, install-record refresh, and dry-run/update behavior contracts.
notes: Implemented ApplyPluginUpdateOperation runtime helper with OpenClaw-style outcome taxonomy (updated/unchanged/skipped/error), target filtering, npm-source/spec gating, dry-run semantics, and persisted install-record refresh for updated plugins. Added tests for mixed-source target handling, dry-run behavior, and non-dry-run persistence of version/installedAt updates.

title: Wire plugin lifecycle runtime methods into control/admin surfaces
description: Expose plugins.install/plugins.uninstall/plugins.update over schema, admin /call, and Nostr control RPC, persisting config mutations through docsRepo/configState.
notes: Added plugin lifecycle method constants/requests/normalizers/decoders and supported-method exposure. Wired ServerOptions + dispatchMethodCall for plugins.install/plugins.uninstall/plugins.update and integrated control RPC switch + runtime handlers in swarmstrd. Runtime handlers persist config via docsRepo/configState and map missing-plugin uninstall to not-found semantics. Added schema/admin/control dispatch tests for new methods.

title: Add runtime backend validation and safe side-effects for plugin lifecycle
description: Harden plugin runtime operations with source/spec validation, installPath defaults, safe uninstall file deletion semantics, and improved npm update status behavior.
notes: Implemented runtime hardening in plugin handlers: install now validates sourcePath/spec and populates default installPath by source; uninstall attempts safe file removal only for managed non-path installs; update now treats pinned npm versions as unchanged and reports stable outcomes for dry-run/execute paths. Added/extended control-RPC tests for valid path installs, missing sourcePath validation, and pinned-version unchanged update behavior.

title: Implement real npm/archive installer execution backend
description: Replace TODO stubs in plugin install/update runtime with real subprocess execution: npm install/update via os/exec, and archive extraction (.tar.gz, .zip) via stdlib archive packages, with zip-slip protection and resolved-metadata propagation.
close_reason: Completed real npm/archive installer execution backend with tests
notes: Created new package internal/plugins/installer with installer.go (Installer interface + factory + ResolveManagedPath/EnsureDir helpers), npm.go (installNPM/updateNPM via npm install --prefix --no-audit --no-fund --save, resolves version+integrity from node_modules package.json + npm ls --json + package-lock.json), archive.go (extractTarGz/extractZip with zip-slip-safe safeJoin, top-level strip for npm tarballs, decompression bomb limits 1GiB/10k files). Replaced TODO blocks in applyPluginInstallRuntime (npm: real install + metadata patch-back; archive: real extraction) and applyPluginUpdateRuntime (npm: validates managed installPath, real update, unchanged semantics on version match). Added installer_test.go covering tar.gz/zip extraction, top-level strip, zip-slip rejection, unsupported format, EnsureDir. Full go test ./... passing.

title: Drop-in parity: OpenClaw config schema + runtime compatibility program
description: Master epic for achieving true OpenClaw drop-in replacement parity. Covers config schema expansion, migration tooling, plugin runtime, skills runtime, tool catalog, WS events, multi-agent, and channel framework needed for a real migration from an existing OpenClaw deployment.
notes: Analysis: Swarmstr method surface is ~82% declared (many stubbed). Config schema is ~15% of OpenClaw scope (missing agents, channels, skills, providers, memory, session, heartbeat, cron, secrets, update, tts, wizard, pairing sections). Plugin runtime is ~5% (metadata only, no execution). Skills is ~10% (no workspace scanning). Channels is ~5% (Nostr DM only). Overall drop-in readiness: ~25-30%. Key gaps ranked: (1) ConfigDoc schema expansion, (2) config format bridge+migration, (3) config redaction, (4) plugin runtime bridge, (5) skills runtime, (6) tool catalog profiles, (7) WS events, (8) multi-agent workspace, (9) channel system.

title: [Critical] ConfigDoc schema expansion to match OpenClaw config sections
description: Expand Swarmstr ConfigDoc to cover all top-level OpenClaw config sections that are currently missing or untyped: agents (named agent configs with model/workspace/toolProfile/memory settings), channels (web/telegram/discord/etc configs), skills (entries with enabled/apiKey/env, global prefs), providers (LLM provider configs and API keys), memory (backend configs), session (history limits, cleanup), heartbeat (delivery targets, intervals), cron (job definitions), secrets (secret store config), update (auto-update config), tts (provider configs), wizard (onboarding config), pairing (pairing settings). Currently these live in ConfigDoc.Extra as untyped map, breaking schema validation and config.schema output.

title: Add typed agents config section to ConfigDoc
description: Add Agents []AgentConfig to ConfigDoc with fields: id, name, model, workspaceDir, toolProfile (minimal/coding/messaging/full), memory settings, heartbeat target. Update config.schema, config.set/patch/apply to support agents.* key paths. Add validation.

title: Add typed providers config section to ConfigDoc
description: Add Providers map[string]ProviderConfig to ConfigDoc with fields: apiKey, baseURL, model, enabled, options. Update config.schema and config.set/patch key paths. Add redaction markers for apiKey fields.

title: Add typed skills, session, heartbeat, cron, tts, secrets sections to ConfigDoc
description: Add remaining strongly-typed top-level config sections: Skills (entries map, global prefs), Session (historyLimit, cleanupPolicy), Heartbeat (target, intervalMs), Cron ([]CronJobDef), TTS (provider, enabled, rate, voice), Secrets (store type, paths). Update schema, set/patch key paths, and validation for each.

title: Add channels config section and schema projection
description: Add Channels map[string]any to ConfigDoc for per-channel configs (web, telegram, discord, signal, iMessage, etc.). Ensure config.schema projects per-channel keys. Add channel-specific key paths to config.set/patch.

title: [Critical] Config format bridge and migration tooling
description: OpenClaw uses JSON5/YAML disk files as its canonical config format. Swarmstr uses Nostr-backed replaceable events. For a user to migrate from OpenClaw to Swarmstr, tooling must exist to: (1) read an OpenClaw config file, (2) convert it to Swarmstr ConfigDoc format, (3) publish it to Nostr relays. Also needs reverse export (Nostr→file) for debugging. Must handle all config sections including sensitive field redaction/restoration.

title: OpenClaw JSON5/YAML config file reader
description: Implement internal/config/openclaw_import.go that reads and parses an OpenClaw config file (JSON5 or YAML format), validates structure, and produces a Swarmstr ConfigDoc. Handle field mapping for: dm.policy←channels.web.dm, relays, agent, providers, plugins, skills, agents, session, heartbeat, cron, tts, secrets.

title: Migration CLI command: config import/export
description: Add swarmstr config-import --file <openclaw-config.json5> command that reads OpenClaw config, converts to ConfigDoc, publishes to Nostr relays. Add swarmstr config-export --out <file> command that reads from Nostr and writes a local JSON/YAML file. Include --dry-run flag to preview conversion without publishing.

title: Migration guide documentation
description: Write docs/MIGRATION_FROM_OPENCLAW.md covering: prerequisites, step-by-step migration (generate Nostr keypair, run config-import, verify with config.get, migrate plugins, test DM flow), known differences (Nostr persistence vs disk files, channel setup differences, plugin execution differences), rollback steps.

title: [Critical] Config response redaction (sensitive field filtering)
description: OpenClaw redacts sensitive config values (API keys, secret tokens, passwords) from config.get responses, replacing them with placeholder markers. Swarmstr currently returns raw config including any sensitive values stored in ConfigDoc.Extra. Must implement: (1) a sensitive-field registry (paths like providers.*.apiKey, skills.entries.*.apiKey, channels.*.token etc.), (2) redaction on config.get output, (3) redaction-aware merge for config.patch (restore redacted values from stored config before writing), (4) tests.

title: [High] Plugin runtime execution bridge (Go→Node.js subprocess)
description: OpenClaw loads TypeScript plugin modules at runtime via jiti and executes them in-process. Swarmstr has no plugin execution layer — it only stores plugin metadata in Nostr config docs. For drop-in parity, Swarmstr needs a bridge that can: (1) load installed npm/path plugins from installPath, (2) start a Node.js subprocess for each plugin (or a shared host process), (3) communicate tool calls/results, hook invocations, and gateway method calls over stdio/JSON-RPC or Unix socket, (4) report plugin status (loaded/disabled/error). This is the single largest functional gap.

title: Design plugin host protocol (Go↔Node.js IPC)
description: Design the JSON-RPC or line-delimited JSON protocol between Go plugin host and Node.js plugin subprocess. Define: initialize (send config, plugin id), tool.call (invoke a tool with args, receive result), hook.invoke (fire a hook, receive response), gateway.call (route a gateway method, receive response), plugin.status (health/ready query). Define error envelope, timeout semantics, and graceful shutdown.

title: Go plugin host runtime (internal/plugins/runtime)
description: Implement internal/plugins/runtime/host.go: starts Node.js subprocess for each installed plugin, manages IPC connection, routes tool/hook/gateway calls to correct subprocess, tracks plugin status, handles restart-on-crash. Include plugin lifecycle (start, stop, restart, health-check). Expose PluginHost interface used by agent runtime and gateway dispatcher.

title: Node.js plugin adapter shim
description: Create a minimal Node.js shim (internal/plugins/runtime/shim/index.js) that: loads the actual plugin module (OpenClaw PluginDefinition), calls register(), collects contributed tools/hooks/gateway methods, then enters a JSON-RPC request loop over stdin/stdout. The shim bridges between OpenClaw plugin SDK expectations and the Go host protocol. Must handle ESM and CJS modules.

title: Wire plugin runtime into agent tool dispatch and gateway method dispatch
description: Update agent runtime to call PluginHost for tool invocations from plugin-sourced tools. Update gateway dispatcher to route plugin-contributed gateway methods through PluginHost. Update tools.catalog to return live plugin tools from PluginHost.

title: [High] Skills runtime: workspace scanning and manifest loading
description: OpenClaw's skills are YAML-defined manifests discovered by scanning agent workspace directories. buildWorkspaceSkillStatus() scans filesystem, reads skill YAMLs, evaluates binary requirements, and reports installed/missing/blocked status. Swarmstr currently has no workspace directory concept and no skill manifest loading — skills.status returns only config-level metadata. Must implement: (1) agent workspace directory concept (local path associated with agent), (2) skill YAML manifest loader, (3) binary requirement checker (hasBinary), (4) skills.status that returns real filesystem scan results matching OpenClaw's SkillStatusReport shape.

title: Agent workspace directory configuration
description: Add workspace_dir per-agent config (in agents[*].workspaceDir) and a global default workspace. Expose workspace resolution helper used by skills, tools, and plugin loading. Ensure swarmstrd can be started with --workspace-dir flag as a default.

title: Skill YAML manifest loader and binary requirement checker
description: Implement internal/skills/manifest.go: reads skill YAML/JSON files from workspace skills directory, parses SkillEntry (name, description, source, skillKey, requires.bins, requires.anyBins, install[].bins, etc.), checks binary availability via PATH lookup. Implement buildWorkspaceSkillStatus() equivalent that returns SkillStatusReport matching OpenClaw output shape.

title: Wire real skill status into skills.status and skills.bins handlers
description: Update skills.status handler to call workspace scanner instead of returning config-only metadata. Update skills.bins to scan all agent workspace dirs and collect required binaries from manifests. Ensure response shape matches OpenClaw's SkillStatusReport exactly.

title: [High] Tool catalog runtime: profiles and plugin tool registration
description: OpenClaw's tool catalog has: (1) a full profile system (minimal/coding/messaging/full) with per-tool profile membership, (2) per-tool labels/descriptions from authoritative definitions, (3) runtime plugin tool resolution that actually loads plugin tools. Swarmstr has a hardcoded static list with no profile resolution and no runtime plugin tool loading. Must implement: profile-aware tool catalog generation, per-tool profile membership tracking, and integration with plugin runtime (swarmstr-20.4) for plugin tools.

title: [Medium] Gateway WS events: active push for all GATEWAY_EVENTS
description: OpenClaw pushes 18 named event types over WebSocket to connected clients. Swarmstr currently only pushes connect.challenge and presence. Missing active push for: tick (periodic), health (health state changes), heartbeat (heartbeat delivery), agent (agent run progress/completion), chat (chat message delivery), cron (cron job fire/result), node.pair.requested/resolved, device.pair.requested/resolved, voicewake.changed, exec.approval.requested/resolved, update.available, shutdown (graceful shutdown notification), talk.mode. Each needs an event struct, emission trigger point in daemon, and fan-out to subscribed WS clients.

title: WS event bus and client subscription model
description: Add a central WS event bus in the daemon that WS runtime can subscribe clients to named event types. Implement Publish(eventType, payload) that fans out to all subscribed clients. Wire into existing WS runtime subscription model (events.subscribe/unsubscribe already implemented at method level, need daemon-side event source wiring).

title: Emit tick, health, heartbeat, shutdown events
description: Implement periodic tick event (configurable interval, default 30s), health state-change events (daemon health transitions), heartbeat delivery events (when heartbeat fires), and graceful shutdown event (before daemon exits). Wire into WS event bus.

title: Emit agent, chat, cron, node/device pairing, exec approval, voicewake, update events
description: Wire remaining domain events into WS event bus: agent run start/progress/completion events from agent runtime, chat message delivery events from DM bus, cron job fire/result events from cron registry, node.pair.requested/resolved from pairing lifecycle, device.pair.requested/resolved from device pairing, exec.approval.requested/resolved from approval registry, voicewake.changed when voicewake state changes, update.available when update check finds new version.

title: [Medium] Config base_hash conflict detection alignment
description: OpenClaw uses content-hash based optimistic locking for config mutations (config.apply, config.patch, config.set require base_hash matching current hash). Swarmstr uses version + Nostr event ID for conflict detection. Clients built for OpenClaw expect base_hash semantics. Must: (1) add base_hash computation for config.get responses (SHA256 of serialized config), (2) add base_hash validation on mutation methods as an alternative to expected_version, (3) document the difference for new clients.

title: [Medium] Multi-agent workspace and runtime
description: OpenClaw supports named agents with per-agent workspace directories, model configs, tool profiles, and separate session/memory spaces. Swarmstr has agent CRUD in Nostr docs but no actual per-agent runtime differentiation (all DMs go to the default agent). Must implement: per-agent workspace directory resolution, per-agent model/tool-profile config, DM routing to correct agent based on peer identity or session key, per-agent session and memory isolation.

title: [Medium] Daemon restart semantics after config changes
description: OpenClaw triggers a gateway restart via SIGUSR1 after config.apply/config.set changes that require restart (relay changes, plugin changes, model changes). Swarmstr hot-applies config changes. For drop-in parity, must: (1) identify which config changes require restart vs hot-apply, (2) implement a restart request queue that executes after responding to the config mutation, (3) optionally support --restart-delay-ms param that OpenClaw clients send, (4) emit shutdown event before restart.

title: [Lower] Channel system framework (web/telegram/discord bridge)
description: OpenClaw has 8+ inbound channels (web, telegram, discord, signal, iMessage, LINE, WhatsApp, Slack) each as a plugin contributing config schema, gateway methods, and message routing. Swarmstr only has Nostr DM. For drop-in parity, the channel system needs a framework that: (1) defines a ChannelPlugin interface in Go, (2) allows channels to contribute config schema sections, (3) routes inbound messages from any channel through the agent runtime, (4) routes outbound replies back to the correct channel. Initially bridge existing channels via the plugin runtime bridge (swarmstr-20.4). Native Go channel implementations can follow.

title: [Lower] Parity acceptance test suite against real OpenClaw instance
description: Create an integration test harness that runs both an OpenClaw instance and a Swarmstr instance side-by-side, sends identical gateway method requests to both, and asserts response shape equivalence. Cover: config.get/set/patch, tools.catalog, skills.status, sessions.list, chat.send, status.get. This provides the ultimate drop-in validation signal.

title: [Superseded] Channel system framework (web/telegram/discord bridge)
description: Originally planned for Telegram/Discord/Signal channel parity. Superseded by direction clarification: Swarmstr is Nostr-native and does not target legacy chat platform channels. Nostr DM is the primary channel. Future channel work (if any) would be other Nostr-native transports, not third-party platform bridges.
close_reason: Out of scope: Swarmstr is Nostr-native. Legacy channel bridges (Telegram/Discord/Signal/etc) are not a goal. Nostr DM is the primary channel.

title: [Revised] Nostr-native channel config section (replaces legacy channel configs)
description: Originally planned for Telegram/Discord/etc channel configs. Revised: Swarmstr channels are Nostr transports. Add a typed Nostr channel config section to ConfigDoc covering: DM policy (existing), NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs (private messages), NIP-28 public channels (group chat), NIP-29 relay-based groups, and custom relay-filtered subscriptions. Each entry specifies subscribe filters and reply routing. This replaces the OpenClaw multi-platform channel config concept with a Nostr-first equivalent.

title: [Revised] Config: hybrid disk+Nostr sync (JSON5/YAML on disk, synced to relays)
description: Revised approach: Swarmstr config should support BOTH a local JSON5/YAML disk file (same format as OpenClaw, for tooling compatibility and human readability) AND Nostr-backed persistence as replaceable events (for cross-device bootstrap and relay-native storage). The two should stay in sync: writes go to disk AND publish to Nostr; on startup, load from disk if present, otherwise bootstrap from Nostr relays. This gives: (1) OpenClaw config file format compatibility, (2) Nostr bootstrap for headless/device deployments, (3) human-editable local config, (4) relay-replicated config across devices. Required: JSON5 parser in Go (use github.com/titanous/json5 or similar), YAML parser, config sync engine (file watcher → Nostr publish, Nostr event → file write), hash-based conflict detection.

title: JSON5/YAML config file reader and writer
description: Add internal/config/file.go with LoadConfigFile(path) that reads JSON5 or YAML config files (auto-detect by extension) and unmarshals into ConfigDoc. Add WriteConfigFile(path, cfg) that writes JSON5. Use github.com/titanous/json5 for JSON5 parsing (pure Go). This makes Swarmstr able to read existing OpenClaw config files directly.

title: Config sync engine: disk↔Nostr bidirectional sync
description: Add internal/config/sync.go: (1) on config write, publish to Nostr relays AND write to local file, (2) on startup, load from local file if present, otherwise fetch from Nostr (bootstrap), (3) file watcher (fsnotify) that re-publishes to Nostr when local file is modified externally, (4) hash-based conflict detection: if Nostr event is newer than local file by timestamp, offer merge. Expose ConfigSyncer used by swarmstrd.

title: Migration CLI: swarmstr config-import / config-export
description: Add swarmstr config-import --file <openclaw-config.json5> that reads an OpenClaw config file and publishes it to Nostr (also writes local copy). Add swarmstr config-export --out <file> that reads from Nostr and writes a local file. Include --dry-run. Write docs/MIGRATION_FROM_OPENCLAW.md.

title: [Revised] Plugin runtime: Goja JS engine (Nostr-native) + optional Node.js compat bridge
description: Revised plugin execution architecture for Nostr-native design. Three tiers: (1) PRIMARY: Native Go plugins — compiled in or loaded via Go plugin interface, full stdlib, best performance. (2) SECONDARY: Goja JS plugins — single-file bundled ES2015+ JS executed in embedded goja runtime (github.com/dop251/goja). Plugins use a swarmstr-plugin-sdk shim that exposes Go-backed Nostr APIs. Crucially, Goja plugins can be published AS NOSTR EVENTS (kind 30plugin or similar) and fetched/loaded from relays at runtime — making the plugin system itself Nostr-native. (3) OPTIONAL COMPAT: Node.js subprocess bridge for running existing OpenClaw TypeScript plugins that require Node built-ins — off by default, requires Node.js installed. This architecture is cleaner, more secure, and Nostr-native vs a pure Node.js subprocess approach.

title: Design swarmstr-plugin-sdk API surface (Go-backed JS APIs)
description: Define what Go APIs are exposed to Goja JS plugins via the plugin SDK shim. Required: nostr.publish(event), nostr.subscribe(filter, cb), nostr.sign(event), config.get(key), config.set(key, value), memory.search(query), sessions.list(), agent.send(sessionKey, text), log.info/warn/error. Optional: http.fetch(url) polyfill, crypto.* polyfills. Design the SDK as a Go struct that Goja VMs are initialized with, defining the JS-visible API contract.

title: Goja plugin runtime (internal/plugins/runtime/goja_host.go)
description: Implement the Goja-based plugin host: (1) load plugin bundle JS (from disk installPath or from Nostr event content), (2) initialize Goja VM with plugin SDK APIs, (3) call plugin's register(sdk) or activate(sdk) export to collect tools/hooks/gateway methods, (4) implement tool.call dispatch (invoke JS function, marshal args/result), (5) hook.invoke dispatch, (6) gateway method routing. Handle VM panics, timeouts (via goja interrupt), and plugin isolation (separate VM per plugin). Expose PluginHost interface matching existing tool/gateway dispatch contracts.

title: [Optional] Node.js subprocess compat bridge for OpenClaw TypeScript plugins
description: Implement optional compat tier: starts a Node.js subprocess for TypeScript plugins that require Node built-ins (fs, http, child_process, etc.) or that cannot be bundled for Goja. Protocol: line-delimited JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout. Node.js shim loads the plugin module via require/import, calls register(), then enters a request loop. Off by default; activated when plugin installRecord has source=npm and a node_modules structure that Goja cannot handle. Requires Node.js in PATH.

title: Wire Goja plugin host into agent tool dispatch, gateway dispatch, tools.catalog
description: Update agent runtime to call PluginHost for tool invocations from Goja plugins. Update gateway dispatcher to route plugin-contributed gateway methods through PluginHost. Update tools.catalog to enumerate live tools from PluginHost (replacing static hardcoded list for plugin section). Update plugins.install to also trigger Goja VM initialization.

title: [Nostr-native] Plugin distribution via Nostr events
description: Design and implement a Nostr-native plugin distribution system that makes plugins first-class Nostr citizens. Plugins are published as parameterized replaceable events (kind 30plugin or using NIP-51 list concept): content = bundled JS (esbuild output), tags = [plugin-id, version, description, capability-tags]. Swarmstr can: (1) search Nostr relays for available plugins by capability tag, (2) fetch a plugin event and install it directly (no npm registry needed), (3) verify plugin event signature before execution (only run plugins from trusted pubkeys), (4) auto-update plugins when a newer event version is found. This is the genuinely novel Nostr-native capability that goes beyond OpenClaw.

title: Define Nostr plugin event kind and tag schema
description: Design the event kind and tag schema for Nostr-distributed plugins. Proposed: kind 30617 (parameterized replaceable, plugin bundle). Tags: ['d', plugin-id], ['v', semver], ['name', display-name], ['description', ...], ['cap', tool|hook|gateway, ...] (capabilities), ['runtime', goja|node], ['hash', sha256-of-content] (integrity). Content: base64 or raw bundled JS. Publish draft NIP or internal spec. Decide on content size limits vs external URL reference approach for large plugins.

title: Plugin registry: search, fetch, verify, and install from Nostr
description: Implement internal/plugins/registry/nostr_registry.go: SearchPlugins(ctx, filter PluginSearchFilter) fetches kind-30617 events from relays, FetchPlugin(ctx, pluginID, pubkey) fetches a specific plugin event, VerifyPlugin(event) checks signature + hash integrity, InstallFromEvent(ctx, event) extracts bundle JS, writes to installPath, creates install record in config. Add swarmstr plugin-search and swarmstr plugin-install-nostr CLI commands.

title: Plugin publisher CLI: bundle and publish plugin to Nostr
description: Add swarmstr plugin-publish --entry <src/index.ts> --id <plugin-id> --version <semver> command that: (1) runs esbuild to bundle the plugin to a single JS file, (2) signs and publishes the bundle as a kind-30617 Nostr event to configured relays, (3) outputs the event ID for sharing. This enables the Nostr-native plugin distribution workflow: write plugin → bundle → publish → anyone can install by event ID or plugin-id+pubkey.

title: [Nostr-native] NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs as primary private channel
description: Currently Swarmstr uses NIP-04 encrypted DMs (kind 4) which leak metadata (participants visible). NIP-17 uses gift-wrapped events (kind 1059) that hide sender/recipient from relay observers. This is the recommended Nostr standard for private agent-to-user messaging. Implement NIP-17 send/receive in dm_bus.go as the preferred privacy mode, with NIP-04 as legacy fallback. This is a Nostr-native improvement that OpenClaw cannot do.

title: [Nostr-native] NIP-29 relay-based group chat as a Nostr channel
description: Implement NIP-29 relay-managed group subscriptions as a Swarmstr channel type. An agent can join a NIP-29 group on a relay and respond to messages addressed to it. This provides a Nostr-native equivalent to Discord/Telegram group channels without requiring those platforms. Implementation: subscribe to kind 9/11 events in a group, route to agent runtime, reply as kind 9 in the group.

title: JSON5/YAML config file reader
closed_reason: implemented internal/config/file.go with LoadConfigFile/WriteConfigFile + full test suite (14 tests passing)

title: Config sync engine (disk↔Nostr)
closed_reason: implemented internal/config/sync.go: SyncEngine with fsnotify watcher, BootstrapFromRelay, PushToRelay, PushToDisk, debounce loop

title: Config response redaction
closed_reason: implemented internal/config/redact.go Redact() with whole-section and field-pattern rules; wired into MethodConfigGet in main.go; 7 tests passing

title: Goja plugin SDK API surface design
closed_reason: implemented internal/plugins/sdk/api.go (Host, NostrHost, ConfigHost, HTTPHost, StorageHost, LogHost, AgentHost interfaces + Manifest/ToolSchema/InvokeRequest/InvokeResult types)

title: Goja plugin runtime
closed_reason: implemented internal/plugins/runtime/goja_host.go: LoadPlugin compiles JS, wires all SDK namespaces, reads manifest, returns GojaPlugin with Invoke(); 10 tests passing

title: NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs
closed_reason: implemented internal/nostr/runtime/nip17_bus.go (NIP17Bus mirroring DMBus API) + DMTransport interface (dm_transport.go) satisfying both buses; wired into main.go via cfg.EnableNIP17 flag in BootstrapConfig; all signatures updated to use DMTransport
